title: Basic enum
instruction:
Define an enum with variants. Use `::` to construct a variant.

Run this.
successMessage: match on an enum must cover all variants — the compiler enforces exhaustive patterns.

title: Enums with data
instruction:
Enum variants can carry data of different types:

```rust
enum Shape {
    Circle(f64),
    Rectangle(f64, f64),
}
```

Run this.
successMessage: Each variant can carry different data. Pattern matching extracts the values. This is Rust's algebraic data types.

title: Option<T> — no null
instruction:
`Option<T>` is either `Some(value)` or `None`. It replaces null.

Run this.
successMessage: Some(5.0) wraps the result. None represents absence. The type signature tells callers this function might fail — they cannot ignore it.

title: Unwrapping Option
instruction:
`unwrap()` extracts the value from `Some`, panicking on `None`.

Use it only when you are certain the value is Some.

Run this.
successMessage: first() returns Option because the vec might be empty. unwrap() is fine when you know it is Some. Use expect() to give a better panic message.

title: if let with Option
instruction:
`if let Some(x) = option` destructures Some without needing a full match.

Run this.
successMessage: if let is idiomatic for 'do this if the option has a value'. Cleaner than a full match when you only care about one variant.

title: unwrap_or and unwrap_or_else
instruction:
Provide a fallback instead of panicking:

Run this.
successMessage: unwrap_or gives a default. unwrap_or_else takes a closure — useful when the default is expensive to compute.

title: Option methods: map and and_then
instruction:
`map` transforms the inner value. `and_then` chains Options.

Run this.
successMessage: map transforms inside Some, leaving None unchanged. and_then chains operations that might fail. These are the functional foundations of Option.

title: Nested Option with ?
instruction:
In functions returning Option, use `?` to propagate None automatically.

Run this.
successMessage: ? on an Option returns None immediately if the value is None. Otherwise it unwraps Some. This makes chains of optional operations concise.
